meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve can't deploy apps (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Template to Solve Deployment Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship professional SaaS apps faster than ever before.
content.answer_block: Deployment paralysis is a silent killer for freelancers. The Antigravity project template solves this by providing a pre-configured, production-ready architecture that abstracts away infrastructure complexity. By pairing it with Claude Code, you automate the CI/CD pipeline, allowing you to focus on building features rather than wrestling with server configurations.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, your client is waiting, and your app is stuck in the 'it works on my machine' graveyard. You’ve built a beautiful interface, but the transition from local development to a live URL has become a recurring nightmare of environment variables, build errors, and cryptic cloud provider logs.

It doesn't have to be this way. The difference between a struggling freelancer and a high-earning AI builder isn't just coding speed—it's the architecture you start with. By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you bypass the friction of manual configuration and move straight to shipping, turning deployment from a bottleneck into a competitive advantage.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Environment
steps.description: Start with a clean slate by cloning the Antigravity template to ensure your local environment mirrors production architecture perfectly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity production-ready template with standard TypeScript and PostgreSQL configurations.

steps.title: Configure Infrastructure via Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scan your project requirements and automatically generate the necessary environment variable schemas to prevent runtime errors.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project requirements and generate a robust .env.example file that covers all necessary cloud provider API keys.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate environment variable schema and validate against project config'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Scripts
steps.description: Define the deployment pipeline within the template to ensure consistent, push-to-deploy functionality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standardized deployment script that performs linting, unit tests, and build optimization before pushing to the target environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create deployment workflow script for Vercel/AWS integration'

steps.title: Validate Production Readiness
steps.description: Run a final audit on your project structure to identify potential bottlenecks before the first production push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current project for production-ready best practices, specifically focusing on error handling and security headers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'run production audit and suggest performance improvements'

steps.title: Execute the First Deployment
steps.description: Trigger the automated pipeline to move your code from local to live, ensuring all assets are optimized for speed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Finalize all configurations and execute the deploy command while logging the output for future debugging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'execute deployment sequence and verify live URL'

pro_tips: Always define your database schema using the template's built-in migration tool to avoid manual SQL drift.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your API endpoints before you even deploy the first version.
Keep your environment secrets in a dedicated vault rather than committing them, even in the .env.example files.
Use the Antigravity 'hot-reload' feature during development to see production-like rendering in real-time.
Automate your client updates by having Claude Code generate a changelog directly from your Git commit history.
content.next_steps: Once your first app is deployed, focus on building a custom component library within your Antigravity template to speed up your next three client projects.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your infrastructure and start scaling your freelance business with a proven, repeatable architecture. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
